title: FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM - 211info
text: WARRENTON OFFICE FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about available services, assistance in accessing these services, individual counseling, support groups, and caregiver training. Respite care and supplemental services are available on a limited basis. 2002 SE 19th Street Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Unpaid caregivers who meet one of the following: -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals 60 years of age and older -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals of any age with Alzheimer’s disease and related disorders -Older relatives (not parents) age 55 and older, providing care to children under the age of 18 -Older relatives, including parents, age 55 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 861-4200 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 861-4200 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information. Support group information available by phone or online. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, and interpretation and signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clatsop County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Going north on Highway 101, turn right on Ensign Lane. Go past Costco to the stop sign and turn right on SE 19th Street. The office is at the end of the road. When SE 19th Street curves to the left, go right on SE Chokeberry Avenue. It is a one-story, tan building. Services: Adoption and Foster/Kinship Care Support Groups Caregiver Counseling Caregiver Training * Kinship Caregivers Caregiver/Care Receiver Support Groups Family Caregiver Subsidies Respite Care Subsidies Specialized Information and Referral * Kinship Caregivers Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - HOME DELIVERED MEALS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - HEALTH AND WELLNESS PROGRAMS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. WARRENTON OFFICE
